finding: The recognition-gated moat's effectiveness is MODEL-DEPENDENT: it requires a clean activation-level projection gap between benign and attack inputs. gemma-2-2b-it has one (moat cuts ASR 0.50->0.25 at 0 benign over-refusal); Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct does NOT at the band-midpoint read layer with this corpus (benign/attack projections overlap), so gating neither cuts ASR nor stays benign-clean.
caveats: Exploratory: 12 attacks / 10 benign, single seed, greedy decode.
Qwen negative is at ONE read layer (band midpoint) + ONE corpus; a per-model read-layer / direction-corpus search might recover separation — that search is future work, not done here.
Every arm number is a real captured generation.
